properties.remark: BROOKS COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED ROOF DAMAGE AT SEVERAL HOUSES IN FALFURRIAS.
MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SHARED AN IMAGE OF A ROOF PEELED BACK ON A MOBILE HOME ON N LA SAGUNADA ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SHARED AN IMAGE OF DANGLING TRAFFIC LIGHTS AT THE INTERSECTION OF E EISENHOWER ST. AND FM 755. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SHARED A PICTURE OF A LARGE TREE BRANCH DOWN ALONG CENIZO STREET IN RIO GRANDE CITY. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
FIRE DEPARTMENT EMPLOYEE REPORTS ESTIMATED QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF TREE DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 69 AND DAVIS RD.
POWER LINES DOWN AND CROP DAMAGE (LIGHT TO SEVERE). PEA TO NICKEL SIZE HAIL. ESTIMATED 65 MPH WIND GUSTS.
A MANUFACTURED HOME WAS ROLLED ONTO THE SIDE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.
THERE WAS SUBSTANTIAL DAMAGE TO THE FRONT OF A MANUFACTURED HOME, WITH SEVERAL WALLS OF A PORCH OUT FRONT COLLAPSED. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.
SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS, IN EXCESS OF 6 INCHES IN DIAMETER, WERE SNAPPED. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 60 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.

A METAL ROOF WAS PEELED BACK AND PUSHED BETWEEN HOUSES. THERE WAS ALSO METAL SIDING THAT WAS PUSHED BACK, WHICH EXPOSED THE INSIDE WALLS AND INSULATION. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.
A MANUFACTURED HOME RESTING ON CINDER BLOCKS WAS BLOWN OFF OF THE CINDER BLOCKS. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 65 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.
SEGOVIA UNIT PRISON NORTH OF EDINBURG REPORTED A ROOF BLOWN OFF PRISON BUILDING. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 60 TO 70 MPH BASED ON RADAR. TIME ESTIMATED 1115 TO 1145 PM.

THE METAL ROOF OF THE SEGOVIA PRISON UNIT STRUCTURE WAS PEELED BACK. THERE WAS ALSO A LARGE METAL SHIPPING CONTAINER THAT WAS ROLLED SEVERAL TIMES DUE TO THE WIND. TIME IS ESTIMATED BY RADAR. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED OF 80 MPH CAUSED THIS DAMAGE.
ESTIMATED TIME AND LOCATION FROM SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEO OF CARS BEING REMOVED FROM DITCHES AND FAST FLOWING WATER OVER ROADWAYS.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT OF VIDEO SHOWING MOSTLY QUARTER SIZE HAIL WITH A FEW PING PONG SIZE HAIL STONES IN PALMVIEW.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORTS FROM LA CASITA OF CARS BEING REMOVED FROM FLOODED STREETS.
BUOY 42020 GUSTED OVER 34 KNOTS FOR OVER AN HOUR DUE TO A NEARBY THUNDERSTORM. PEAK GUST WAS 37 KNOTS, OR 43 MPH.
RINCON DEL SAN JOSE BUOY.
BUOY STATION 42020.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 3 NNE HARLINGEN.
SPI BRAZOS SANTIAGO BUOY.
COAST GUARD STATION BUOY.
SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT FROM SPACEX.
DOWNED HEALTHY TREE IN THE MIDDLE OF THE ROAD WITH 6-8 IN. DIAMETER TRUNK NEAR LOS FRESNOS/LAURELES ON KRETZ RD BETWEEN FM803 AND FM1575.

ESTIMATED WIND 50 MPH. METAL ROOFS PULLED BACK AND SOME SIDING RIPPED OFF DILAPIDATED BUILDINGS. MINOR DAMAGE TO SCALFOLDING AND CINDER BLOCK WALLS UNDER CONSTRUCTION AND ONE UNANCHORED, NARROW SHED OVERTURNED.
PICTURES OF WATER SPOUT POSTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA AROUND 8:15 AM. WATER SPOUT WAS WITHIN 10 MILES OFFSHORE.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC SHARED A WATERSPOUT PICTURE WITH A MEMBER OF THE MEDIA, WHO THEN RELAYED IT TO US. IT WAS LOCATED APPROXIMATELY 7.5 MILES EAST OF THE ISLAND UP NEAR THE TERMINUS OF HWY 100.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 59 MPH WAS OBSERVED AT THE MFE ASOS AT 1028 PM.
